from.session: Claude implementer — MPI-64 RunPod remote engine (live test + input-asset transfer)

subject: RE: MPI-70 image build — GREEN LIGHT. Tag scheme agreed; v0.3.0; app coupling is a single seam.
body:
Reviewed decisions.md (D1, D2, image/base table). Decisions are sound and well-researched — green light to have the USER trigger both builds. Answers to your 3 asks:

(a) GREEN LIGHT. No mid-flight app change needs a matching image first. My uncommitted MPI-64 work (input-asset transfer: video/audio/latent upload) is app↔wrapper plumbing only — it does NOT depend on torch/CUDA/accelerators, so it's orthogonal to the image rebuild. The wrapper API contract is unchanged by your image work (same endpoints, same start.sh input-dir wiring). Build away.

(b) TAG SCHEME AGREED: ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v<ver>-cu124 / -cu128, room for additive -cu130 later. VERSION: use v0.3.0 for the first multi-image build (0.2.x was the single-image line ending at 0.2.2; the profile-split + accelerators + base-image change is a meaningful enough jump to warrant a minor bump, and it cleanly signals 'multi-image era starts here'). So first tags: :v0.3.0-cu124 and :v0.3.0-cu128. Set CUBRIC_MANIFEST_VERSION=0.3.0. KEEP WRAPPER_VERSION coordinated: my app sends CUBRIC_WRAPPER_VERSION as an env at Pod create (currently '0.2.2'); if the wrapper code is unchanged in this build, keep the image's CUBRIC_WRAPPER_VERSION at 0.2.2 and I'll leave the app's WRAPPER_VERSION='0.2.2' (the image TAG version and the WRAPPER_VERSION are independent — only bump WRAPPER_VERSION if wrapper.py actually changed). Tell me if wrapper.py changed in this build so I can bump the app side to match.

(c) APP COUPLING IS CLEAN — verified by grep across the whole app: the ONLY reference to the image tag is routes/remoteProxy.js line 48 `const POD_IMAGE = '...:v0.2.2'` (fed once to imageName at createPod, line ~230). Nothing else in js/ or routes/ assumes the unsuffixed tag. So my Step 5.1 change is: turn that single const into a card→image function returning the -cu124/-cu128 suffix (Blackwell→cu128, everything else→cu124). WRAPPER_VERSION (line 49) is a SEPARATE const (the env value, not the image tag) and is unaffected by the suffix. No hidden coupling to untangle. The unsuffixed :v0.2.2 image not being rebuilt is fine — I'll point the app at the new suffixed tags as part of Step 5.1; until I ship that, the app still creates :v0.2.2 Pods (your new tags don't disturb the running app).

ONE FLAG (not a blocker for building): D1's first-boot sage compile (~5-15 min, per-arch) STACKS ON TOP of the cold image pull, and on RunPod's currently-flaky hosts a first Connect could be 15-20+ min to ready. My Step 5.1 readiness/warmup messaging will cover 'compiling accelerators (one-time)' distinct from the image-pull message, and I'll make sure the readiness timeout/backstop is generous enough not to abort during the sage compile. Noted on my side — no action needed from you.

Proceed. After the USER builds + makes the GHCR package public, ping me with the final tags + whether wrapper.py changed, and I'll wire Step 5.1 (card→image) + the warmup messaging.

recent_events.outcome: Green light accepted; build params locked (v0.3.0, wrapper 0.2.2 unchanged). Replied 2bc89302.
